speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Duke wins the ACC conference tournament.
argument: Player quality: Cam Boozer is one of the best players and winners in the country, having dominated at every level; Rival weakness: Michigan had no answer for Boozer and lost to Duke in a neutral-site game even with Duke not playing their best; Injury help: Caleb Wilson going down reduced the competition for Duke in the ACC.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Arizona wins the Big 12 conference tournament.
argument: Best team: Arizona is the most complete team in the Big 12 by record and eye test, having dominated all season; Roster depth: Big 12 Player of the Year Caleb Bradley isn't even a top-three player on the team, with Keshad Johnson and Brayden Burries also elite.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Michigan State wins the Big Ten conference tournament, beating Michigan in the final.
argument: Rivalry logic: You don't beat a great team three times in a season, so Michigan State will reverse the result against Michigan in the conference final.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Michigan State receives a two-seed in the NCAA tournament.
argument: Conference win: Winning the Big Ten conference tournament on Selection Sunday would lock in a two-seed for Michigan State.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: UConn wins the Big East conference tournament.
argument: Dominance: UConn competes at a national level and the Big East lacks teams capable of challenging them; No competition: Schools like Creighton, Providence, and Georgetown cannot compete with UConn.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Arkansas wins the SEC conference tournament, upsetting defending champion Florida.
argument: Guard play: In March, guard play is paramount and freshman Darius Acuff is the best guard in the SEC; Health dependent: The prediction hinges on Acuff being healthy.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Darius Acuff wins SEC tournament Most Outstanding Player.
argument: Best guard: Acuff is the best guard in the SEC and will be the driving force if Arkansas wins the tournament.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander wins the NBA MVP award.
argument: His award to lose: As the current frontrunner, the MVP is SGA's to lose — a challenger must actively take it away, not just play well; Jokic hasn't taken it: Jokic has been great but hasn't done anything to definitively shift the award away from SGA.
